task: Check what happens when an agent searches for tools via the MCP server: query the production API at /api/tools/search for 'auth', 'payments', 'database', 'email', 'monitoring'. For each, check: does the top result have install_command? Does it have a useful tagline? Is the category correct? Report which queries give poor first impressions to agents.

assignments.mcp: Query the production API at https://indiestack.ai/api/tools/search for each of these 5 queries: 'auth', 'payments', 'database', 'email', 'monitoring'. For each query, inspect the TOP result and check: (1) does it have a non-empty install_command field? (2) does it have a useful, descriptive tagline (not generic/empty)? (3) is the category field correct/relevant for the query? Compile a report table showing query, top result name, install_command (yes/no/value), tagline quality (good/weak/missing), category correctness (correct/wrong/missing). Flag which queries give poor first impressions to AI agents and why.

decomposition.reasoning: This is a pure API audit task — querying the production search endpoint and evaluating response quality. It only needs the MCP/Integration department since it's about testing the external API that agents consume. No frontend, backend code changes, or devops work needed. Single department, single group.

strategy_review.reasoning: This is a cheap, high-value audit of the core product. The MCP search endpoint is how agents actually discover tools — it's the front door. The 02:19 task audited individual tool DETAIL responses (specific slugs like 'supabase'). This audits the SEARCH endpoint (keyword queries like 'auth'). Different endpoint, different concern, no overlap. If an agent searches 'auth' and gets a result with no install command or a garbage tagline, that's a failed conversion on the primary distribution channel. With 53 users, every agent interaction needs to land. Cost will be ~$0.15-0.30 (5 curl requests + analysis). Read-only, zero deployment risk.

approved_tasks.mcp: Query the production API at https://indiestack.ai/api/tools/search for each of these 5 queries: 'auth', 'payments', 'database', 'email', 'monitoring'. For each query, inspect the TOP result and check: (1) does it have a non-empty install_command field? (2) does it have a useful, descriptive tagline (not generic/empty)? (3) is the category field correct/relevant for the query? Compile a report table showing query, top result name, install_command (yes/no/value), tagline quality (good/weak/missing), category correctness (correct/wrong/missing). Flag which queries give poor first impressions to AI agents and why.

risk_flags: If this reveals problems, the fix tasks need separate approval — this audit should NOT silently turn into a code change session
